content.full_text:
California Fair Political Practices Commission

July 18, 1991

Eula Barnes  
Richmond City Clerk  
City of Richmond  
2600 Barrett Ave.  
Richmond, CA 94804

Re: Combined Campaign Report  
Our File No. I-91-329

Dear Ms. Barnes:

This is to confirm advice provided to your office on July 1, 1991, regarding the campaign disclosure provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").1/ This letter constitutes written advice under Section 84205.

Section 84205 states:

The Commission may by regulation or written advice permit candidates and committees to file campaign statements combining statements and reports required to be filed by this title.

A ballot measure qualification committee submitted petitions to your office on June 28, 1991, to place a measure on the November 4, 1991 ballot. The campaign statement due in connection with the qualification effort (Section 84200.5) would normally cover July 1, 1991, through July 12, 1991, with a filing deadline of July 19, 1991. However, we believe it is appropriate for committees primarily formed to support or oppose the qualification of the ballot measure to combine that report with the semi-annual statement for the first six months of 1991 (Section 84200). Therefore, such committees should file a single campaign statement disclosing contributions received and expenditures made during the period January 1 through July 12, 1991. The deadline for this combined report is July 19, 1991.

1/ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

If you have any questions regarding the above, please do not hesitate to contact me at (916) 322-5662.

Sincerely,

Scott Hallabrin
Acting General Counsel

By: Lynda Cassady
Political Reform Consultant
